flashcards.back: For any node: All values in left subtree < node value < All values in right subtree. Both subtrees must also be BSTs.

flashcards.front: How does search operation work in BST?
flashcards.back: Compare key with current node. If smaller, search left subtree; if larger, search right subtree. Repeat until found or reach null.

flashcards.front: What are the three cases for delete operation in BST?
flashcards.back: 1) Node is leaf: Simply remove. 2) Node has one child: Replace with child. 3) Node has two children: Replace with inorder successor.

flashcards.front: Where is the maximum value located in a BST?
flashcards.back: The maximum value is always found at the rightmost node of the BST.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of BST operations in worst case?
flashcards.back: O(n) when the tree becomes skewed (like a linked list). O(log n) when the tree is balanced.
